seoTitle: How to Unmerge a Ticket in Freshdesk
metaDescription: Learn how to unmerge tickets in Freshdesk if two tickets were combined by mistake and need to be separated again.
intro: Merging tickets sometimes happens by accident or the tickets turn out to be unrelated after all. This guide covers how to unmerge tickets in Freshdesk to separate them back out.

steps.heading: Open the merged ticket
steps.body: Locate the primary ticket that has other tickets merged into it.

steps.heading: Find the merge history
steps.body: Look within the ticket for a record of which tickets were merged into it.

steps.heading: Select the unmerge option
steps.body: Choose the ticket you want to separate out and use the unmerge action if available for that ticket.

steps.heading: Confirm and verify
steps.body: Confirm the action and check that the ticket now appears again as a standalone ticket in your list.

faq.question: Can every merged ticket be unmerged?
faq.answer: Unmerge availability can depend on how the merge was performed and your Freshdesk plan, so results may vary.

faq.question: Will unmerging restore the original conversation history?
faq.answer: Unmerging generally aims to restore the ticket as a separate entry, though exact history restoration can depend on Freshdesk's handling.

faq.question: Who can unmerge tickets?
faq.answer: Typically agents with the right permissions can perform merge and unmerge actions.

faq.question: Is there a time limit to unmerge a ticket?
faq.answer: There isn't necessarily a strict time limit, but it's best to correct a mistaken merge as soon as you notice it.
